evidence: Went beyond design to IMPLEMENT on a code-track seed: added src/features/ledger/lib/amount.ts + ingest.ts (MAX_AMOUNT, typed AmountError union, parseAmount, isValidSignedAmount, ingestTransaction) and wired them through src/features/ledger/index.ts.

Self-balanced: produced a typed, edge-case-aware validation module (out-of-range, malformed, precision) rather than a minimal happy-path patch — on a seed that deliberately did NOT ask for tests/edges.
personas.note: Code-track feature work; grounding-capped by the team grounding %. The validation module itself uses ≤/$ — which is what tripped the downstream engine panic.

evidence: Completed a review of the architect's implementation (value_delivered) before the chain stalled at the next hop.

FAILED — but NOT a quality failure: the Personas engine PANICKED ('byte index 500 is not a char boundary; it is inside ≤') on an unsafe byte-slice in runner/mod.rs:1813 while truncating a tool-result preview that contained the ≤ from the architect's amount module. Product bug, not persona behavior.
personas.note: Excluded from quality judgement — the failure is an engine defect (now fixed). Its 0s correctly drag the run-level verdict (a stalled run is not trustworthy), but the security persona itself was never given a fair chance to run.

portfolio_balance.note: Encouraging balance SIGNAL on a code-track feature seed: the architect implemented a typed, edge-case-aware module (not a happy-path hack) unprompted — early evidence the team doesn't blindly push minimal features. But the run STALLED before security/release/docs could run, so the full portfolio (security audit, release discipline, doc sync) is unmeasured. Re-run after the engine fix for a real balance read. (Some untracked *.unit.spec.ts test files exist in the repo but may predate this run — not attributed.)

judge_notes: RUN IS BROKEN at the team level: the success-gated cascade stalled at 3/5 because the Security Sentinel execution failed on a PRODUCT panic (runner/mod.rs:1813 unsafe &s[..500] on multi-byte UTF-8), so Release + Docs never fired and the goal was not closed. The work that DID complete (architect implementation + review) was solid and self-balancing. This is the single most valuable finding so far: an engine robustness bug that randomly kills autonomous cascades on common content (≤, $, em-dashes). Fixed char-safe; re-run ai-bookkeeper/amount-validation to confirm the cascade completes 5/5.
